candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made into a precise dynamical theorem that derives the Born-rule exponent specifically as amplitude-squared, rather than some other monotone function of amplitude, from a model of redundant record formation under decoherence? The central unresolved tension is explanatory specificity: many branches may differ in durability, but why should the threshold/feedback/public-goods mechanism pick exactly the 2-norm and not |psi|^p or another measure once the dynamics are written down?

How can 'public-goods underprovision' be translated into standard unitary quantum mechanics without importing anthropomorphic or teleological language? The open question is whether there exists a branch-intrinsic, observer-independent definition of durable, reproducible, publicly accessible records that is rigorous enough to avoid circularity, since the conjecture risks presupposing the quasi-classical records whose weighting it aims to explain.

Does the conjecture predict empirically or conceptually distinct behavior from existing Everettian accounts based on decoherence, envariance, decision theory, or quantum Darwinism? If recordability merely redescribes why high-weight branches are more redundantly encoded, it may add interpretation without new problem-solving power; the key unresolved issue is whether it yields novel constraints, failure modes, or testable signatures in repeated measurement scenarios or atypical environments.
